title: Teaching professionals n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the academic, administrative, or auxiliary activities of kindergarten, elementary, or secondary schools. Plan, direct, or coordinate student instruction, administration, and services, as well as other research and educational activities, at postsecondary institutions, including universities, colleges, and junior and community colleges. All education administrators not listed separately. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare students for further education by encouraging them to explore learning opportunities and to persevere with challenging tasks.
Observe and evaluate the performance of other instructors and provide feedback to strengthen teaching skills.
Review instructional content, methods, and student evaluations to assess strengths and weaknesses, and to develop recommendations for course revision, development, or elimination.
Confer with other staff members to plan and schedule lessons promoting learning, following approved curricula, and meet with other professionals to discuss individual students' needs and progress.
Attend professional meetings, conferences, and workshops to maintain and improve professional competence.

Prepare for assigned classes and show written evidence of preparation upon request of immediate supervisors, and prepare objectives and outlines for courses of study, following curriculum guidelines or requirements of states and schools.
Prepare reports on students and activities as required by administration, and maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws or administrative policies.

Select, order, and issue books, materials, and supplies for courses or projects, and recommend, order, or authorize purchase of instructional materials, supplies, equipment, and visual aids designed to meet student educational needs and district standards.

Collaborate with other teachers and professionals in the development of instructional programs and participate in conferences, seminars, and training sessions to keep abreast of developments in the field.
Attend staff meetings and serve on committees as required, and sponsor extracurricular activities, such as clubs, student organizations, and academic contests.
Plan and supervise class projects, field trips, and other experiential activities, and guide students in learning from those activities.
Use various teaching methods, such as lectures, discussions, and demonstrations to instruct students individually and in groups.
Adapt teaching methods and instructional materials to meet students' varying needs, abilities, and interests.
Establish and enforce rules for behavior and procedures for maintaining order among the students for whom they are responsible.
Prepare and administer written, oral, and performance tests and issue grades in accordance with performance, and assign and grade class work and homework.

Conduct classes, workshops, and demonstrations to teach principles, techniques, or methods in subjects, and create a balanced program of instruction that allows students to observe, question, and investigate.

Guide and counsel students with adjustment, academic problems, or special academic interests, and provide information and guidance for course selection, career decisions, and other academic and vocational concerns.
Observe students to determine their qualifications, limitations, abilities, interests, and other individual characteristics to provide effective individualized instruction.
Meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress and to determine priorities for their children and their resource needs.
Provide assistive devices, supportive technology, and assistance accessing facilities to students with disabilities.
Train and assist tutors and community literacy volunteers.
Select and schedule class times to ensure maximum attendance, and participate in publicity planning, community awareness efforts, and student recruitment.
Confer with parents or guardians, other teachers, counselors, and administrators to resolve students' behavioral and academic problems.
Keep informed about trends in education and teaching materials to effectively adapt instructional practices.
Prepare and conduct teacher training programs and workshops designed to promote effective instructional practices and materials.
Determine training needs of students or workers and plan and conduct activities that fulfill curriculum requirements.
Advise teaching and administrative staff in curriculum development, use of materials and equipment, and implementation of state and federal programs and procedures.
Promote the university by participating in community, state, and national events or meetings, and developing partnerships with industry and secondary education institutions.
Prepare reports on academic or institutional data, and provide reports on students and activities as required by administration.
Design or use assessments to monitor student learning outcomes and assess students' progress throughout tutoring sessions.
Create school improvement plans, using student performance data, and develop curricula, recommending revisions and additions as necessary.
Write grants to procure external funding, and supervise grant-funded projects.
Mentor and support administrative staff members, such as superintendents and principals.
Formulate strategic plans for the institution and establish operational policies and procedures, making modifications based on analysis of operations, demographics, and other research information.
Advise students on issues such as course selection, progress toward graduation, and career decisions, and counsel students regarding educational issues, such as class scheduling and registration.
Coordinate and direct extracurricular activities and programs, such as after-school events and athletic contests.
Meet with federal, state, and local agencies to stay abreast of policies and to discuss improvements for education programs.

Participate in special education-related activities, such as attending meetings and providing support to special educators throughout the district, and identify and implement intervention strategies or individualized education plans (IEPs) for students.
Plan and lead professional development activities for teachers, administrators, and support staff, and participate in training and development sessions to improve coaching practices.
Direct, coordinate, and evaluate the activities of personnel, including support staff engaged in administering academic institutions, and supervise grants-funded projects.

Review and approve new programs or recommend modifications to existing programs, submitting program proposals for school board approval as necessary, and evaluate curricula and teaching methods to determine effectiveness and compliance.

Prepare, maintain, or oversee the preparation and maintenance of attendance, activity, planning, or personnel reports and records, and maintain accurate and complete student records as required by administrative policy.

Confer with other academic staff to explain and formulate admission requirements and course credit policies, and participate in student recruitment, selection, and admission, making recommendations as required.
Teach courses within their department and instruct individuals in career development techniques, such as job search and application strategies.
Consult with government regulatory and licensing agencies to ensure the institution's conformance with applicable standards, and observe and evaluate the performance of other instructors.
Determine course schedules, and coordinate teaching assignments and room assignments to ensure optimum use of buildings and equipment.
Direct activities of administrative departments, such as admissions, registration, and career services.
Appoint individuals to faculty positions, and evaluate their performance, and recruit, hire, train, and terminate departmental personnel.

Plan, administer, and control budgets, maintain financial records, and produce financial reports, including preparing budget requests and recommendations, or grant proposals to solicit program funding.

Determine allocations of funds for staff, supplies, materials, and equipment, and authorize purchases, and review registration statistics, consulting with faculty officials to develop registration policies.
Write articles, manuals, and other publications, and assist in the distribution of promotional literature about facilities and programs.
Set educational standards and goals, and help establish policies and procedures to carry them out, and enforce discipline and attendance rules.
Confer with parents and staff to discuss educational activities, policies, and student behavior or learning problems, and meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress.
Organize and direct committees of specialists, volunteers, and staff to provide technical and advisory assistance for programs, and participate in faculty and college committee activities.
Teach classes or courses to students, and provide private instruction to individual or small groups of students to improve academic performance.
Plan and develop instructional methods and content for educational, vocational, or student activity programs, including preparing lesson plans or learning modules based on students' needs and goals.
Operate equipment such as computers or audio-visual aids to supplement presentations, and use computers and audio-visual aids to enhance instruction.
Teach students study skills, note-taking skills, and test-taking strategies, and counsel students with adjustment or academic problems.
Review class material with students through discussion and problem-solving methods, and assist students in academic environments, such as classrooms or laboratories.
Organize tutoring environment to promote productivity and learning, and schedule tutoring appointments with students or their parents.

Monitor student performance or assist students in academic environments, making suggestions for improvement and ensuring satisfaction of course standards and objectives, and conduct follow-up interviews with counselees to determine if needs are met.
Provide feedback to students using positive reinforcement techniques to encourage, motivate, or build confidence, and maintain confidentiality of student assessment results.

Evaluate students’ or individuals’ abilities, interests, and personality characteristics using tests, records, interviews, or professional sources, and review transcripts to ensure that students meet graduation or college entrance requirements.
Provide students with information on college degree programs, admission requirements, and financial aid opportunities, and counsel individuals or groups on educational or vocational situations.
Address community groups, faculty, and staff to explain available counseling services, and advocate for new schools or the repair/remodeling of existing facilities.
Participate in publicity planning and student recruitment, and coordinate the production of university publications, such as course catalogs.
Instruct and monitor students in the use and care of equipment, and provide students with disabilities with assistive devices or support technology.
Review and interpret government codes, and develop programs to ensure adherence to codes and facility safety, security, and maintenance.
Research or recommend textbooks, software and equipment to complement tutoring and teaching. Identify, develop, or implement strategies for student improvements and educational success.

Observe students during classroom and play activities to evaluate performance, behavior, social development, and physical health, and supervise students during activities outside the classroom, such as recess and field trips.
Attend meetings, educational conferences, and training workshops, and serve on committees.
Plan and promote career and employment-related programs and events, such as career planning presentations, work experience programs, job fairs, and career workshops.
Establish and enforce administration policies and rules governing student behavior.
Plan, direct, and participate in recruitment and enrollment activities.
Prepare students for later educational experiences by encouraging them to explore learning opportunities and to persevere with challenging tasks.

Supervise, train, and direct professional staff and interns, and confer with parents or guardians, teachers, administrators, and other professionals to discuss children's progress, resolve behavioral, academic, and other problems, and determine priorities for students and their resource needs.
Sponsor extracurricular activities, such as clubs, student organizations, and academic contests.
Identify cases of domestic abuse or other family problems and encourage students or parents to seek additional assistance from mental health professionals.
Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws, district policies, and administrative regulations.
Provide crisis intervention to students when difficult situations occur at schools and refer students to outside counseling services.
